claims.claim: Increasing FRPCM thickness from 1 mm to 2 mm and 3 mm (with constant 1 mm AEGF) reduced the maximum temperature of cell 2 by 67.1°C and 113.8°C respectively, compared to the 1 mm FRPCM battery module.
claims.quote: Compared to the 1 mm FRPCM BM, the highest temperature of cell 2 within the 2 mm and 3 mm FRPCM BMs was decreased by 67.1 ◦C and 113.8 ◦C, respectively.

claims.claim: Increasing FRPCM thickness extended the TR interval between cells 1 and 2, with time intervals of 184 s, 291 s, and 533 s for 1 mm, 2 mm, and 3 mm FRPCM respectively (with constant 1 mm AEGF).
claims.quote: The time intervals for TR between cells 1 and 2 were 184 s, 291 s, and 533 s, while those between cells 2 and 3 were 137 s, 280 s and 493 s.

claims.claim: In Test-6 (3 mm FRPCM + 2 mm AEGF) and Test-7 (1 mm FRPCM + 3 mm AEGF), thermal runaway propagation was completely blocked, with only cell 1 undergoing TR.
claims.quote: Both the structural materials in Test-6 and Test-7 block the TRP. In Test-6, the introduction of a thicker layer of FRPCM led to only cell 1 undergoing TR.

claims.claim: The TR interval between cells 2 and 3 was consistently shorter than the interval between cells 1 and 2, because cell 3 absorbed heat from the heating plate and TR of cell 1 before cell 2 underwent TR.
claims.quote: the TR interval between cells 2 and 3 is consistently shorter compared to the interval between cells 1 and 2. This phenomenon arises from the fact that, preceding the TR of cell 3, the heating plate and the TR of the first cell supplied a considerable amount of heat to the entire BM. Moreover, cell 3 absorbed some of this heat before cell 2 underwent TR, consequently reducing the interval between the final two cells.

claims.claim: Increasing AEGF thickness improved thermal insulation and extended TR intervals between cells, but did not necessarily reduce maximum battery temperature and could even cause temperature increases despite the same FRPCM thickness.
claims.quote: an increase in the thickness of the AEGF results in improved heat insulation between the cells. However, it should be noted that this increase in thickness does not necessarily lead to a better reduction in the maximum battery temperature. In fact, in some cases, it can even cause a temperature increase despite the same thickness of the FRPCM.

claims.claim: The thermal runaway propagation interval within a module is reduced by 72%, from 611 s to 176 s, indicating an accelerating trend due to heat accumulation once thermal runaway initiates.
claims.quote: the thermal runaway propagation interval is markedly reduced by 72%, from 611 s to 176 s, indicating that controlling the initial thermal spread within the module is more critical than mitigating thermal propagation between modules

claims.claim: During thermal runaway propagation, energy dissipated via liquid cooling plates accounts for more than 70% of the total energy, far exceeding heat transfer through neighboring battery surfaces and tab connectors.
claims.quote: during thermal runaway propagation, the energy dissipated via the liquid cooling plates accounts for more than 70% of the total energy, far exceeding the heat transfer through neighboring battery surfaces and the tab connector

claims.claim: Replacing polystyrene insulation with aerogel decreases heat transfer through the rear surface of the battery by 55.3%, significantly slowing thermal runaway propagation.
claims.quote: Replacing polystyrene with aerogel decreases heat transfer through the rear surface of the battery by 55.3%, significantly slowing the progression of thermal runaway propagation

claims.claim: Using a single piece of 13 mm thick aerogel positioned between the third and fourth cells accelerates the overall thermal runaway propagation time by 46.2% compared to evenly distributed aerogel.
claims.quote: using a single piece of 13 mm thick aerogel accelerates the overall TR propagation time by 46.2%

claims.claim: Replacing the air gap with aerogel between modules delays the thermal runaway onset moment for adjacent modules by only 36 s and 49 s because over 70% of heat flows through the bottom cooling plate from module to module.
claims.quote: Replacing the air gap with aerogel will slightly delay 36 s and 49 s of the TR onset moment for module 5 and module 7, respectively. The insulation performance of aerogel is limited since over 70% of heat flows through the bottom cooling plate from module to module

claims.claim: The average maximum temperature during thermal runaway of NMC-811 cells was 899.3 °C, significantly higher than that of LFP cells at 524.4 °C.
claims.quote: The average maximum temperature of the NMC-811 cells is 𝑇 𝑇 𝑅,𝑚𝑎𝑥,𝑁𝑀𝐶811 = 899.3 ◦C (𝜎𝑁𝑀𝐶811 = 38.98 ◦C), and therefore significantly higher than that of the LFP cells with 𝑇 𝑇 𝑅,𝑚𝑎𝑥,𝐿𝐹 𝑃 = 524.4 ◦C (𝜎𝐿𝐹 𝑃 = 5.94 ◦C). The reasons for this are a higher reaction enthalpy and a lower thermal mass than the LFP cells

claims.claim: The maximum temperatures of NMC-811 cells during thermal runaway exceed the melting temperature of aluminum (660 °C), requiring adjacent components to consist of temperature-resistant materials like steel to ensure structural integrity, whereas lighter materials like aluminum can be used in LFP batteries.
claims.quote: The maximum temperatures of the NMC-811 cells exceed the melting temperature of aluminum (𝑇𝑚𝑒𝑙𝑡,𝐴𝑙 = 660 ◦C [52]), requiring that adjacent components of the cell module or battery housing consist of temperature-resistant materials like steel to ensure structural integrity and to avoid unwanted venting gas leaks. By contrast, lighter materials like aluminum can be used in LFP batteries.

claims.claim: The thermal runaway reaction in NMC-811 cells is nine times faster in terms of cell energy compared to LFP cells, with NMC-811 having a reaction velocity of 20.33 Wh/s versus 2.19 Wh/s for LFP.
claims.quote: The thermal runaway reaction in the cell is, therefore, eight times faster in terms of capacity and nine times faster in terms of cell energy, compared to LFP cells with a thermal runaway reaction velocity of 0.66 Ah s−1 and 2.19 Wh s−1, respectively.

claims.claim: The overall thermal runaway propagation velocity in the LFP module is five times lower than in the NMC-811 module, giving LFP a clear safety advantage.
claims.quote: Overall, the total propagation velocity remains lower by a factor of five, which gives the LFP module a clear safety advantage over the NMC-811 module.

claims.claim: NMC-811 cells experienced an average mass loss of 45.8% during thermal runaway, leading to reduced mechanical integrity and increased thermal contact resistance, while LFP cells had a significantly smaller average mass loss of 21.1%, maintaining mechanical integrity.
claims.quote: the NMC-811 cells experience a mass loss of 39.6–48.7%. This leads to a reduced mechanical integrity and clamping force up to small gaps between the cells, which results in higher thermal contact resistances and slower propagation. On the contrary, mechanical integrity is maintained in the LFP cells with significantly smaller mass losses of 20.9–21.6%.

claims.claim: The venting gas of NMC-811 cells ignited during thermal runaway because the ignition temperature of C2H4 (440 °C) was exceeded at the safety vents, while the venting gas of LFP cells did not ignite as the ignition temperature was not reached.
claims.quote: According to the data from the thermocouples on the safety vents at the NMC-811 cell in Fig. 13(c), the lowest ignition temperature of the venting gas components of C2H4 (440 °C [72]) was exceeded several times, so the cell itself provides enough heat for ignition and acts as an ignition source, which means that ignition of NMC-811 cells is unavoidable in the presence of oxygen. In the case of LFP cells, however, the ignition temperature is not reached, as can be seen in Fig. 13(d), which means that the venting gas does not ignite.

claims.claim: The venting gas temperature during NMC-811 thermal runaway exceeds the melting temperature of PE (100 °C) and PTFE (320 °C), which are common electrical insulation materials for high-voltage busbars, creating a risk of arcing in the high-voltage system.
claims.quote: the venting path should also be insulated from the electrical path or all high-voltage components, as the venting gas temperature exceeds the melting temperature of common electrical insulation materials for high-voltage busbars, as shown in Fig. 13(c) and (d) for the insulation materials PE (𝑇𝑚𝑒𝑙𝑡,𝑃 𝐸 = 100 °C [73]) and PTFE (𝑇𝑚𝑒𝑙𝑡,𝑃 𝑇 𝐹 𝐸 = 320 °C [74]). The lack of electrical insulation can, in turn, lead to arcing in the high-voltage system and thus increase the hazardous potential.

claims.claim: At the pack level, the gravimetric energy density advantage of ternary (NMC/NCA) batteries over LFP batteries decreases from 34% at cell level to 16% at pack level, because LFP batteries have lower integration losses (20%) compared to ternary batteries (37%).
claims.quote: At the cell level, the average gravimetric energy densities are 252 Wh kg−1 for ternary and 167 Wh kg−1 for LFP batteries. This 34% advantage can be partially transferred to the pack level. With an average energy density of 158 Wh kg−1 for ternary and 133 Wh kg−1 for LFP batteries including all integration and safety measures, the advantage is still present but decreases to 16% because the losses due to the integration of the cells into the battery pack are lower for LFP batteries at 20% than for ternary batteries at 37%.

claims.claim: At the pack level, the volumetric energy density advantage of ternary batteries over LFP is completely eliminated, with average pack-level volumetric energy densities of 241 Wh/L for ternary and 243 Wh/L for LFP, due to higher integration losses of 62% for ternary versus 40% for LFP.
claims.quote: With an average energy density of 241 Wh L−1 for ternary and 243 Wh L−1 for LFP batteries including all integration and safety measures, the average energy densities at pack level are almost identical due to the integration losses in energy density of 62% for ternary batteries and 40% for LFP batteries.

claims.claim: Side wall ruptures occurred in NMC-811 cell cans due to temperatures above the melting point of aluminum, causing unintentional gas leakage and preventing venting exclusively via intended channels, while no structural loss or side wall ruptures were observed in the LFP module.
claims.quote: it can be seen that although the initial venting occurs through the intended vent in the lid assembly, side wall ruptures also happen due to the high temperatures above the melting point of the aluminum cell can... In contrast, Fig. 15(b) and (d) show no visually detectable structural loss of the LFP cell module. All cells released venting gas exclusively through the safety vents, and no side wall ruptures are evident.

claims.claim: The five-minute thermal propagation safety requirement of GB 38031-2020 can likely be met without additional measures for LFP cells, as only 107.25 Ah and 360 Wh would undergo thermal runaway in that period, whereas the complete five-cell NMC-811 setup would fully propagate within the same period.
claims.quote: Regarding the five-minute requirement for the thermal propagation test in the GB 38031-2020 standard [9], these requirements can easily be achieved with LFP cells, as only 107.25 Ah and 360 Wh will go in thermal runaway during this period with a measured average propagation velocity of 0.36 Ah s−1 and 1.20 Wh s−1. For this reason, the safety requirement can probably even be fulfilled without other measures. On the other hand, the complete, tested five-cell NMC-811 setup would propagate through in this period.

claims.claim: The thermal runaway triggering temperature for NMC-811 cells was 371.6 °C at the front surface with 33.9 °C at the back, while LFP cells triggered at a significantly higher temperature of 436.6 °C at the front with 41.6 °C at the back, due to the higher thermal stability of the LFP cathode.
claims.quote: This point was reached in the NMC-811 module at a surface temperature of 371.6 °C. The thermal runaway of the first LFP cell was reached significantly later with a temperature of 436.6 °C, which can be justified by the significantly higher thermal stability of the LFP cathode due to their strong and thermally stable P-O bonds and inhibition of oxygen loss... the energy balance between heat generation and dissipation within the cell comes out of equilibrium at temperatures of 371.6 °C at the front and 33.9 °C at the back of the NMC-811 cell. Analogous, the LFP cell goes into thermal runaway at temperatures of 436.6 °C at the front and 41.6 °C at the back of the cell.

claims.claim: The cell voltage is not always a suitable thermal runaway detection criterion for slowly propagating cell chemistries or cells connected in parallel, because the voltage does not completely collapse and may remain within the normal permissible operating range.
claims.quote: This phenomenon illustrates the complexity of detecting thermal runaway via the cell voltage, as the voltage does not completely collapse in the case of slowly propagating cell chemistries or cells connected in parallel. The measured values are still in the normal permissible operating range of the cell, making it difficult to distinguish them from an intentionally high current load. The cell voltage is, therefore, not always a suitable detection criterion, and other redundant detection methods should be taken into account

claims.claim: The exponential increase in time span between successive thermal runaways in the NMC-811 module is attributable only to the pause time component, not to the internal thermal runaway reaction time, due to increasing thermal contact resistance from mass ejection reducing mechanical integrity.
claims.quote: Considering the internal propagation times and the subsequent pause time, this exponential increase is only found in the pause time of the NMC-811 cells, which reinforces the assumption.

claims.claim: The total venting gas production rate for NMC-811 cells is significantly higher than for LFP cells, with NMC-811 having a mean production rate of 2.44 L/Ah compared to 0.56 L/Ah for LFP cells, allowing pressure relief elements and venting channels in LFP battery systems to be dimensioned significantly smaller.
claims.quote: the mean production rate for NMC-811 cell chemistries of 2.44 l ⋅ Ah−1 is used for the following calculation. For LFP cells, significantly lower values between 0.57–1.12 l Ah−1 have been reported in the literature... the pressure relief elements and venting channels in LFP battery systems can be dimensioned significantly smaller than in NMC battery systems, as the flow rate is significantly lower and can be easily compensated.
